mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive association with INDPRO increases (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 220.0 signal accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the negative predictive relationship vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150,000 confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer income growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when payroll growth exceeds 150,000, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% over consecutive 1-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint indicator loses predictive power when GDPC1 and INDPRO diverge, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (t-stat < 1.8) in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% alongside stable CPIAUCSL, ultra-loose monetary conditions provide liquidity support that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or the predictive accuracy falls below chance (50%) during periods of rising inflation expectations.
